summary.intent: Migrate the web UI from vanilla JavaScript with HTM tagged templates to TypeScript with JSX/Preact, add type safety improvements (enums, typed RPC, typed WebSocket events), and ensure all tests pass.
summary.outcome: Full TypeScript migration completed with Vite build pipeline, 142 TS/TSX files replacing 135 JS files, 0 tsc errors, local-validate mostly passing (248/253 e2e tests pass), though the preserveModules refactor attempt was reverted and the shim system remains.

repo: The project uses Askama for HTML templates — there are two copies of templates: crates/web/src/templates/ (used at runtime by Askama) and crates/web/src/assets/ (static copies that were stale). Always update templates/ not assets/.

E2E tests use playwright and dynamically import individual JS modules (e.g. import('js/state.js')). With Vite bundling these no longer exist as separate files, requiring either shims or preserveModules.
The i18n system uses dynamic imports with paths like './locales/en/common.js' — after renaming locale files to .ts, these paths must be updated to .ts or Vite won't bundle the locale chunks.

The local-validate.sh script runs multiple parallel checks: biome (lint+format), tsc (type checking), fmt (Rust fmt), i18n parity, file-size (1500 line limit, was Rust-only, now includes TS/TSX), e2e tests, zizmor (GHA security), install-names, install-docs, and lockfile.
The file-size check in check-file-size.sh only covered .rs files initially; it was extended to cover .ts and .tsx files as part of this migration.
nextest.toml can configure per-crate thread limits to prevent flaky tests under load — moltis-qmd uses fake shell scripts that time out under high parallelism.
The original JS used window globals like window.__voiceOnboardingSaveSettingsRequest as test hooks; these need to be re-exposed after bundling.
The e2e helpers.js file derives asset prefix by finding script[type='module'][src*='js/app.js'] — any app entry point must match this pattern.
Committing dist/ (built output) is intentional so cargo build works without Node.js. Source maps were disabled to reduce binary size from 27MB to 11MB.
The share page (share.html, share-app.mjs, share-theme-init.js) is a standalone page not bundled by Vite — it must keep its own vendor luxon.mjs.

code.finding: Dynamic imports use './locales/${lng}/common.js' — must be .ts extension after migration or Vite won't bundle locale chunks, causing t() to return raw keys
Must expose window.__moltis_state (state module) and window.__moltis_modules (all modules tests import) for e2e shim compatibility
Template needs both the real dist/main.js entry AND a compat js/app.js script tag so e2e helpers can find the app via querySelector
E2E shim that proxies state module functions (setConnected, setWs, setChatSeq, etc.) from window.__moltis_state for tests that do import('js/state.js')
Added [[profile.default.overrides]] with filter='package(moltis-qmd)' and max-threads=1 to prevent QMD shell script tests from timing out under load
Must be .cjs extension (not .js) because package.json has type:module, which makes .js files ES modules that can't use require()
New file with {type:commonjs} to override parent package.json's type:module for the e2e directory, allowing require() in spec files
VanillaConfirmDialog must use provider-modal-backdrop/provider-modal CSS classes (not Modal component's modal-overlay) — e2e tests look for .provider-modal locator
Three entry points: app (src/app.tsx), login-app (src/login-app.tsx), onboarding-app (src/onboarding-app.tsx). Output to dist/ with no source maps.

workflow: When using git rebase and accepting 'ours' for conflict resolution, file splits (where large files are split into sub-modules) need to be re-done after rebase since the trimming of parent files is lost.

Biome's a11y rules (useButtonType, noLabelWithoutControl, noStaticElementInteractions, useKeyWithClickEvents) are now enforced on TSX that was previously unanalyzable HTM — these should be disabled in biome.json to match pre-migration behavior.
Running git add -A can accidentally pick up uncommitted changes from other branches/sessions on disk — use specific file paths when staging.
The GPG/YubiKey signing flow requires the pinentry dialog to get focus; background tasks cannot successfully sign commits.
When subagents convert large files, they sometimes create stub implementations instead of fully porting complex components — always verify sub-components are complete by running e2e tests.

preserveModules:true in Vite rollupOptions outputs each source file as its own module — cleaner than shims but requires careful handling of entry point naming and emptyOutDir conflicts with static files.

friction: YubiKey/GPG signing required physical tap for every commit — multiple commits failed in background tasks, requiring manual user intervention repeatedly.
Rebase conflict resolution repeatedly lost file splits (parent files reverted to pre-split full content), requiring re-splitting 7 large files multiple times.

Subagents creating stub implementations ('full TSX conversion pending') instead of actually porting complex JS components (ChannelStep, VoiceStep, RemoteAccessStep in onboarding) — caused 17+ e2e failures.
The i18n locale .js→.ts extension bug caused ALL translations to silently fail (t() returned raw keys like 'actions.cancel') with no obvious error in tsc or Vite build.
preserveModules Vite experiment introduced 9 new e2e regressions and had to be reverted — the interaction between preserveModules, entryFileNames, and the static js/ files was complex.
E2E tests dynamically import individual JS modules that no longer exist as separate files after Vite bundling — required creating 14+ shim files and exposing all modules on window.__moltis_modules.
The moltis-qmd tests have a 5-second timeout for shell script execution that's too tight when running under full test suite load (4267 tests), causing flaky failures.
The git add -A in early commits accidentally included uncommitted Rust files from other in-progress work, requiring a revert commit to clean up.
open_items: Switch to Vite preserveModules:true to eliminate all 14+ shim files and make e2e tests import real bundled modules directly — requires solving emptyOutDir/static file conflict
Migrate remaining imperative DOM pages (registerPage + raw DOM) to proper Preact component trees to eliminate the remaining ~134 as-HTML*Element casts from querySelector/closest
Refine ~32 remaining Record<string,unknown> in dynamic data contexts (channel configs, ws frames, gon data) with proper discriminated union types
The /clear e2e test is flaky (passes alone, sometimes fails in full suite) — investigate race condition in chat sequence reset
agents.preset.save RPC test fails — likely a backend issue with the preset save endpoint, not a frontend issue
Provider modals (~2200 lines of imperative DOM in providers/) are the last major component area not converted to Preact — still uses createElement patterns with 139 DOM calls
The shim files (js/state.js, js/helpers.js, etc.) are a maintenance burden — each new function added to a module needs to be added to the corresponding shim
SettingsPage.tsx is still 6885 lines — exceeds the 1500-line limit but the TS file size check wasn't catching it (need to verify check-file-size.sh covers all TS files)
